svg.aria-label: Animated diagram: keys enter the in-memory MemTable, are flushed to L0 files when it fills, and are merge-sorted by background compaction into a single sorted L1 SSTable per bucket, where duplicate keys collapse and tombstoned keys are dropped.
svg.text: Keys (and their value-log pointers) flush from memory into L0 files, then compact into one sorted L1 SSTable per bucket.

g.text: put(a) → the key + its u128 value-log pointer land in the in-memory MemTable (a skip list).
More writes. Only keys and pointers live here — the values are already in the value log.
The MemTable hits 95% capacity → it is sealed read-only and a fresh one takes over writes.
Flush: the sealed MemTable is written out as one sorted L0 file for the bucket.
New writes fill the next MemTable — including an update to a, which does not touch the L0 file.
Second flush → a second L0 file. L0 files overlap: a now exists in two of them.
delete(b) is a tombstone — a marker that shadows older versions, not an in-place erase.
Third flush. Three L0 files for this bucket → the compaction threshold is reached.
Compaction: a background worker merge-sorts every L0 file of the bucket together with L1.
One sorted L1 SSTable per bucket. Duplicate a collapses to its newest version; b's tombstone drops the key.
The L0 files are deleted: 7 L0 entries became 4 L1 entries — compaction reclaims the overlap.
The cycle repeats. A read checks MemTable → L0 → L1, and one bucket's L1 is 1/16th of the keyspace.

svg.text: KEYS HELD PER TIER, OVER TIME
Illustrative sizes. A ✗ cell is a tombstone; a dotted cell is an empty slot.
